name: The Digital Agency Show | Helping Agency Owners Transform Their Business Mindset to Increase Prices, Work Less, and Grow Profits
description:
Helping digital agency owners transform their business mindset so they can increase prices, work less, and grow profits.
A weekly podcast where host Brent Weaver chats with today's most successful entrepreneurs running digital agency businesses in the web design, web development, and digital marketing industry.
  
Brent also chats with high-profile entrepreneurs and other smart people that can help listeners grow their digital agency.
Each interview deep dives into the emotional details of going

recent_episodes.title: Episode 376: The End of UGURUS – Last Episode with Brent Weaver and Jen Buzza
recent_episodes.description: In this final episode of the Digital Agency Show, host Brent Weaver gets interviewed by UGURUS Lead Mentor, Jennifer Buzza. They talk about the shutdown of UGURUS after 12 great years, lessons learned from the business and recording 375+ episodes, and much more. Tune in for this Brent's final thoughts on a great run.

recent_episodes.title: Episode 375: From Solo to Scalable: The Power of White Labeling for Agencies with Michael Knorr
recent_episodes.description: Michael Knorr is the CEO of That! Company, a white-label digital marketing agency that has helped over 700 agencies scale their businesses by providing SEO, PPC, and digital marketing services under their clients’ brands. With nearly two decades of experience, Michael has built a business model that allows agency owners to grow without getting bogged down in execution—freeing them up to focus on strategy, client relationships, and revenue growth.

recent_episodes.title: Episode 374: Smarter PPC Strategies & The Future of Paid Ads with John Horn
recent_episodes.description: John Horn is the CEO of StubGroup, a digital advertising agency specializing in expert PPC management for businesses of all sizes. Since joining the company as employee number one, John has helped StubGroup grow into a leading boutique agency, managing over 15,000 campaigns and generating more than 3 million leads and $400 million in revenue for clients.

score_reason: Perfect fit for Andreas's agency building and exit story, targeting digital agency owners who would value his 16-year journey from startup to Canada's largest independent food agency.

key_themes: Scaling agencies from solo/small teams to multi-million dollar businesses
Profitability, pricing strategy, and working less while earning more
Building systems, operations, and remote/distributed teams
AI and technology adoption for agency efficiency and client results
enrichment.relevant_episode: E362: Agency Growth Made Simple: Trust, Teamwork, and Execution with Tim Kelsey — Both discuss scaling operations and building high-performing teams across distributed regions, though Andreas brings the unique angle of traditional industry transformation and AI-driven decision-making.
enrichment.coverage_gap: How AI can help agencies *sell better* and *understand clients deeper* (the say-do gap) — most recent AI episodes focus on hiring/recruitment and operations, not client psychology and data-driven insight generation for agency pitch and strategy work.
enrichment.suggested_hook: The agency owners trying to scale are using the same outdated gut instincts their clients use — here's how one founder went from Ogilvy to building Canada's largest food agency by teaching clients (and agencies) to let AI show them what customers *actually* do, not what they say.
